data.title: review-complexity-classifier.sh: classifier exits 1 on small doc-only diff -> fallback to standard tier silently
data.description:
When running REVIEW-WORKFLOW Step 3 (classify review tier) for a 2-file diff (1 deletion from .test-index, 3-line change to plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md), review-complexity-classifier.sh exits with code 1.

Command used (via COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md):
  bash plugins/dso/scripts/review-complexity-classifier.sh

Diff summary:
   .test-index                             | 1 -
   plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md | 3 +--
   2 files changed, 1 insertion(+), 3 deletions(-)

Actual Behavior:
  - review-complexity-classifier.sh exits 1 (failure)
  - REVIEW-WORKFLOW failure invariant triggers: defaults to standard tier (dso:code-reviewer-standard, sonnet model)
  - No diagnostic output explaining why the classifier failed

Expected Behavior:
  - Classifier should either successfully classify the tier or emit a specific error message explaining the failure cause
  - A small, doc-only, deletion-dominated diff should be trivially classifiable (likely light tier)

Impact:
  - Silent fallback to standard tier means over-review for simple changes
  - No signal to diagnose or fix the underlying classifier error
  - ARTIFACTS_DIR=/tmp/workflow-plugin-1d67cd84373f7be6 (session where failure occurred)
  - Diff file: /tmp/workflow-plugin-1d67cd84373f7be6/review-diff-26b9dee3.txt
